All Classes and Interfaces

Class
Description
AircraftCarrier3D
Aircraft carrier: the largest ship in the fleet, occupies 4 cells.
BattlefieldView3D
Full 3D battlefield: both boards (the player's and the machine's) exist in the same scene at all times, placed apart from each other, floating over one continuous "sea" slab, inside a box "sky" made of ordinary walls (not a SubScene fill gradient -- see BattlefieldView3D.createGradientTexture(javafx.scene.paint.Color, javafx.scene.paint.Color, int, int) for why).
BattlefieldView3D.Turn
Whose board the camera should be looking at.
Board
A 10x10 Battleship board: the grid of Cells, the fleet placed on it, and the rules for placing ships and resolving shots.
BoardLabels3D
Builds the column-letter (A..J) and row-number (1..10) labels that sit just outside a board's two edges, like a spreadsheet's headers.
BoardLabels3D.Board
Which board these labels are for -- picks which quad texture mapping to use.
BoardLabels3D.Layout
Which edges the letters/numbers sit on, and which direction each one reads in.
BoardListener
Receives notifications about changes to a Board.
BoardListenerAdapter
No-op default implementation of the model's BoardListener, following the same Adapter pattern as ShipPlacementAdapter and CombatAdapter.
BoardView3D
3D view of the 10x10 board.
Cell
A single cell of a Board: its current CellState, and -- if a ship occupies it -- a reference to that ship, used to look up hit/sunk information without scanning the whole fleet.
CellAlreadyShotException
Thrown when a shot targets a cell that has already been shot at (miss, hit, or sunk).
CellState
The state a single board Cell can be in.
CombatAdapter
No-op default implementation of CombatListener, following the same Adapter pattern as ShipPlacementAdapter.
CombatController
Handles HU-2 (the human player firing at the machine's board) and HU-4 (the machine automatically firing back).
CombatListener
Receives notifications about the combat phase (HU-2, HU-4): whose turn it is, and when the game ends.
Config3D
Shared scale constants and reusable materials for all 3D shapes on the board (water, ships and shot marks).
DemoBattlefield3D
Demo class: NOT part of the final game.
DemoShapes3D
Demo class: NOT part of the final game, it only serves to preview the full fleet (1 aircraft carrier, 2 submarines, 3 destroyers, 4 frigates) already placed on the 3D board, and to visually verify that "hit" and "sunk" marks render correctly.
Destroyer3D
Destroyer: medium-sized ship, occupies 2 cells.
FleetPlacer
Places a complete fleet of ships onto a board at random valid positions, following the rules (no overlaps, nothing hanging off the edge).
FleetViewBuilder
Builds the Ship3D shapes matching every Ship already placed on a model Board -- used for the machine's fleet (placed all at once by FleetPlacer, then mirrored into the scene in one pass) and reusable anywhere else a board's ships need a matching set of 3D shapes.
Frigate3D
Frigate: the smallest ship in the fleet, occupies 1 single cell.
Game
Orchestrates one full match: the two players, whose turn it is, and the rule that a hit or a sunk ship grants another shot while a miss passes the turn (HU-2).
Game.ShotOutcome
The position targeted and the result of one resolved shot, returned by Game.playMachineShot() since -- unlike Game.fireAtMachine(Position) -- the caller doesn't already know which position the machine chose.
GamePhase
Which phase the single main screen is currently showing.
GameScreenController
The controller behind main-view.fxml: the single screen the whole game runs on.
HoverMarker3D
A white ring that hovers just above a board cell, used to highlight whichever cell the mouse is currently over.
HumanPlayer
The human player.
HuntShootingStrategy
A smarter alternative to RandomShootingStrategy: while there is no damaged-but-not-yet-sunk ship on the board, it behaves exactly like plain random targeting.
InvalidShipPlacementException
Thrown when a ship cannot be placed where requested -- either it would overlap another ship already on the board, or part of it would fall outside the board's bounds.
KonamiCodeDetector
Watches keyboard input for the Konami Code (Up, Up, Down, Down, Left, Right, Left, Right, B, A) and runs a callback once the whole sequence is entered correctly, in order -- this is what activates HU-3's "cheat" reveal of the machine's fleet.
MachinePlayer
The computer-controlled opponent (HU-4).
Main
Application entry point.
MainGameController
The single entry point that wires the whole game together.
OrbitCameraRig3D
A free-roaming "dome" camera: the user can drag to orbit around a fixed target point at a constant radius -- geometrically, a point moving over the surface of a hemisphere centered on the target (the concept-sketch idea: pick a fixed radius from the target, and every point at that radius traces out the dome the camera is allowed to move on).
Orientation
Orientation in which a ship can be laid out on the board.
OutOfBoardException
Thrown when a requested row/column falls outside the board (0-9 for a 10x10 board), before a valid Position can even be built.
Player
A participant in a game: a nickname and the Board holding their own fleet (the board the opponent shoots at).
Position
An immutable board coordinate, expressed as a zero-based row and column pair (0-9 for a 10x10 board).
RandomShootingStrategy
Picks a uniformly random cell, among the ones not shot at yet, on every call -- the machine-player behavior required by HU-4.
SavedGameRepository
Persists a Game to disk (HU-5), split across two files: saved-game.ser -- the whole Game object, written with plain Java serialization.
SegmentState
Visual state of an individual segment (cell) of a ship.
Ship
A ship placed on a Board: a type, a starting Position, an Orientation, and per-segment hit tracking.
Ship3D
3D representation of a fleet ship, built from JavaFX Shape3D subclasses (Box, Cylinder, Sphere).
ShipFactory
Creates Ship instances and the list of ship types a complete fleet is made of.
ShipFactory3D
Factory for 3D ships.
ShipPlacementAdapter
No-op default implementation of ShipPlacementListener.
ShipPlacementController
Handles HU-1: letting the human player place their fleet manually, one ship at a time, by clicking a cell on their own board.
ShipPlacementListener
Receives notifications about the fleet-placement phase (HU-1).
ShipType
The four kinds of ship in a fleet, each with a fixed size (number of cells it occupies) and a fixed count per player, as specified by the project statement: 1 aircraft carrier, 4 cells 2 submarines, 3 cells each 3 destroyers, 2 cells each 4 frigates, 1 cell each A full fleet therefore occupies 20 cells in total.
ShootingStrategy
Decides where the machine player shoots next.
ShotMark3D
Builds the 3D marks placed on a cell after a shot.
ShotResult
The outcome of resolving a single shot against a Board.
Submarine3D
Submarine: occupies 3 cells.
Turn
Whose turn it currently is to shoot.
Water3D
Represents a single water cell of the board (an empty cell with no ship).
WreckageDebris3D
A scattered cluster of broken hull fragments with fire and smoke, shown on a cell where a ship has been hit -- replaces the old plain scorch mark with something that reads as an actual wreck.
WreckageDebris3D.FragmentSpec
One fragment: position, size, and how it's tipped over.
